text: Please create a mnemonic for me

Sure! Think of the microcontroller as a little boss on a chip: it receives power (positive and negative), turns the lights (3 V) on or off at its fingers (pins), and tells you exactly when that happens—controlled by its program in C++. This was you remember: microcontroller = little boss that controls everything with power and programs!

text: Can you make it even simpler for me?

Sure! A microcontroller is like a small switch box: power in, buttons (pins) on or off, and a program tells you when each button should light up. This way you remember: power + buttons + program = microcontroller!

text: Can you explain to me in detail how a microcontroller works?

Sure! A microcontroller is a small computer on a chip that executes a program. It needs power, so a positive and negative pole. It can output a voltage of 3 V via its pins, which it can switch on (“HIGH”) or off (“LOW”). It switches the pins exactly according to the program, which is written in C++ or created with Blockly, for example. This allows the microcontroller to control other devices by sending signals to the pins – all very quickly and precisely.
